sn8.12:1.1: On one occasion the Venerable Vangīsa was dwelling at Sāvatthī in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's Park. Now on that occasion the Venerable Vangīsa had only recently attained arahantship and, while experiencing the happiness of liberation, on that occasion he recited these verses:[n.528] Vv. 753–57 are considerably more compressed than the partly parallel verses at Th 1253–67. For a concise comparison of the two versions, see Ireland, Vaṅgīsa, pp. 7-8.
